annotation_target.structure: Near-end antecedent — bass in deepest register, resigned melody
teaching_goals: the piece has arrived at complete resignation
the RH chords have no upward aspiration — pure acceptance
style_tips: the character is now one of serene acceptance

teaching_notes.note: The RH chords have lost their upward yearning — they simply are.
technique: flat, even touch — no phrase shaping needed here

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 33–36 of Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4, view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 33–36 of the Prelude — the piece is approaching its conclusion. Describe it.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 33–36.
Measures 33–36 are near the end of the descent — the bass is in its deepest register and the RH melody has lost its upward aspiration entirely. The mood is final and resigned. Let me loop mm. 33–36.
Chopin Prelude E minor mm. 33–36: near-conclusion, bass in deepest register, resigned RH melody.
